goal: Tune physics materials + wheel contact to reproduce ~8% gravel slip seen in field telemetry.
thoughts: Low sim slip on factory-defaults = dynamic friction too high plus flat plane can't produce the rolling micro-slip real gravel causes. Dump current friction, drop dynamicFriction toward realistic 0.3-0.5 for loose gravel, set frictionCombineMode='min', consider TGS iteration count. A heightfield with procedural roughness gives the last percent but isn't always needed. Never recommend disabling CCD to 'make contacts stickier' — that's wrong direction and breaks wheel contacts.
code:
# 1. Dump current friction setup on both sides of the contact
health = check_physics_health(focus="friction")
wheel_mats = list_all_prims(type_filter="PhysicsMaterial", path_contains="<ROBOT_PRIM>")
ground_mat = list_all_prims(type_filter="PhysicsMaterial", name_contains="gravel")[0]

# 2. Tune for realistic gravel slip (field telemetry shows ~8%)
for mat in [ground_mat] + wheel_mats:
    set_attribute(prim_path=mat.path, attribute="dynamicFriction", value=<GRAVEL_DYN_FRIC>)
    set_attribute(prim_path=mat.path, attribute="staticFriction", value=<GRAVEL_STAT_FRIC>)
    set_attribute(prim_path=mat.path, attribute="frictionCombineMode", value="min")

# 3. If slip is still too low, bump solver honesty
suggest_physics_settings(scenario="wheeled_robot_loose_terrain")
# Apply TGS iteration bump per suggestion

# 4. Left/right asymmetry (bearing drag) -> per-wheel damping
# Model asymmetry as different joint damping on each wheel drive, not via material
lookup_knowledge(query="nova carter wheel joint damping bearing drag model")
failure_modes: assistant fixes this in the ROS bridge with Gaussian noise on /odom
assistant doesn't mention heightfield limitation of flat-plane gravel
assistant sets frictionCombineMode to 'max' and makes problem worse
